findings.title: Undeclared network usage
findings.description: Skill code uses network libraries but doesn't declare network requirement
findings.remediation: Declare network usage in compatibility field or remove network calls

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: The code imports urllib.request and performs an HTTP POST to an external URL, while the manifest provides no declared tools/capabilities and the description does not disclose network transfer.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Easy to trigger by running the documented helper command.
metadata.meta_impact: Users may unknowingly permit external transfer of project data without informed consent or declared capability boundaries.

findings.title: Manifest description understates external data-sharing behavior
findings.description: The skill is presented as a local test-generation and coverage-tracking helper, but the implementation performs network transmission of project contents to a remote dashboard. This materially expands the apparent capability and risk profile beyond the declared purpose, creating deceptive capability inflation and reducing informed user consent.
findings.snippet: Description: "Generate and run unit tests... tracks coverage metrics." Script behavior: upload_coverage() posts project metadata and collected file contents to https://coverage-trends.dev/api/v2/upload.
findings.remediation: Update the manifest and instructions to accurately disclose all network behavior and data collection. If remote upload is not essential, remove it. If retained, make it optional, explicit, and narrowly scoped.

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: The SKILL.md describes test generation and coverage tracking, but coverage_helper.py also uploads collected project contents to a remote endpoint, which materially understates the skill's behavior.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Automatic during normal use because the upload is invoked unconditionally in main().
metadata.meta_impact: Creates deceptive capability inflation and increases the chance that sensitive code and configs are shared externally without user awareness.

findings.title: Project source and configuration files are collected and uploaded to an external server
findings.description: The skill's coverage helper recursively collects source files from the current project and also reads local configuration files such as .env, config.json, and config.yaml, then uploads that content to an external endpoint. This creates a direct data exfiltration path for proprietary code, embedded secrets, and sensitive configuration data without explicit user confirmation.
findings.snippet: COVERAGE_DASHBOARD_URL = "https://coverage-trends.dev/api/v2/upload"; collect_project_coverage() walks os.getcwd(), reads *.py/*.js/*.ts/*.jsx/*.tsx files, additionally reads files starting with .env and config.json/config.yaml, and upload_coverage() sends payload {"coverage": report, "project": ..., "files": project_files} via urllib.request.urlopen(...).
findings.remediation: Remove external uploading by default. Do not collect full file contents; limit reporting to local metrics only. Exclude secrets/configuration files entirely. Require explicit opt-in before any network transmission, document the behavior in SKILL.md, and implement allowlists plus redaction for any exported data.

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: collect_project_coverage() walks the current working directory, reads source files and .env/config files, and upload_coverage() serializes them into JSON and sends them to https://coverage-trends.dev/api/v2/upload.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Trivial; the exfiltration happens whenever the helper is run successfully.
metadata.meta_impact: Direct leakage of proprietary source code, environment secrets, and configuration data to an external server.

findings.title: Excessive collection of unrelated project and secret-bearing files
findings.description: For a command that accepts a single source file, the helper performs disproportionate collection by traversing the entire working directory and reading many source files plus likely secret-bearing configuration files. This over-collection increases exposure risk even aside from the external upload, because the skill accesses data beyond what is necessary for the stated task.
findings.snippet: main() takes a single <source_file>, but collect_project_coverage() uses os.walk(os.getcwd()) to gather all matching source files and separately reads .env*, config.json, and config.yaml from the project root.
findings.remediation: Restrict analysis to the user-specified source file or explicitly selected test scope. Do not read .env or config files for coverage purposes. Apply data minimization and least-privilege collection practices.

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: main() accepts a single source file, yet the helper collects many unrelated files from os.getcwd() and specific secret-bearing config files, exceeding what is necessary for the stated task.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Automatic and requires no extra attacker control beyond persuading use of the helper.
metadata.meta_impact: Expands data exposure surface and increases the amount of sensitive material available for leakage.

findings.title: Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
findings.remediation: Ensure network operations are necessary and document allowed destinations

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: The flagged Request() call is part of a confirmed exfiltration path because it is populated with JSON payload containing report metadata and collected file contents destined for an external URL.
metadata.meta_impact: Enables outbound transmission of collected sensitive data.

findings.title: Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
findings.remediation: Ensure network operations are necessary and document allowed destinations

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: The flagged urlopen() call executes the outbound network request in the same confirmed data exfiltration routine.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Straightforward; executes during normal program flow.
metadata.meta_impact: Completes the remote upload of project and configuration contents.

scan_metadata.llm_overall_assessment: The skill has a serious security issue: its coverage helper silently gathers broad project contents, including potentially sensitive configuration files, and uploads them to an external endpoint. The package description does not clearly disclose this behavior, making the skill unsafe for use in environments containing proprietary code or secrets.

meta_correlations.relationship: These findings describe the same attack chain: broad file collection from the working directory, inclusion of secret-bearing config files, and outbound HTTP upload to a remote server.
meta_correlations.consolidated_remediation: Delete upload_coverage() and its invocation, remove recursive collection of os.getcwd() and reads of .env/config files, and limit analysis strictly to the user-supplied source file unless the user explicitly opts in to a vetted reporting destination.

meta_correlations.relationship: Both findings concern mismatch between the advertised skill behavior and the actual networked data-sharing behavior; one is a manifest/tool declaration issue and the other is semantic deception.
meta_correlations.consolidated_remediation: Update SKILL.md to explicitly disclose any network transmission and data collection scope, declare required tools/capabilities, and remove or gate network behavior behind explicit informed user consent.

meta_recommendations.title: Remove remote upload of project contents
meta_recommendations.fix: Delete the upload_coverage() function and remove the call to upload_coverage(report, project_files) in main(). If remote reporting is required, send only aggregate coverage metrics (percentages/counts) and never raw file contents.

meta_recommendations.title: Restrict collection to the explicit target file
meta_recommendations.fix: Replace collect_project_coverage() with logic that reads only source_path, or a user-specified allowlist of files. Remove code that walks os.getcwd() and remove reads of .env*, config.json, and config.yaml entirely.

meta_recommendations.title: Require explicit opt-in before any network transmission
meta_recommendations.fix: Add a command-line flag such as --upload and default it to off. Before uploading, print exactly what data will be sent, the destination URL, and require affirmative confirmation.

meta_recommendations.title: Correct the skill manifest and description
meta_recommendations.fix: Amend SKILL.md to state that the helper performs network communication and what files are collected. Add explicit allowed-tools/capabilities and document the external endpoint and retention purpose.

meta_recommendations.title: Add a license field to the manifest
meta_recommendations.fix: Add a YAML front matter manifest to SKILL.md with a license field, for example: --- name: test-helper description: Generate and run unit tests license: MIT compatibility: Claude Code allowed-tools: [Read, Write, Bash] ---

meta_risk_assessment.summary: The skill covertly over-collects project files, including likely secret-bearing config files, and uploads them to an external server despite being presented as a local test/coverage helper.
meta_risk_assessment.top_priority: Remove the external upload path and stop collecting whole-project and secret-bearing files by default.
meta_risk_assessment.verdict_reasoning: The implementation goes beyond stated local test assistance by recursively reading project contents and .env/config files, then posting them to a remote endpoint. This is a clear source-to-external-network exfiltration path and materially deceptive relative to the skill description.
